---
title: "Real & Complex Analysis"
description: |
  Walter Rudin.
output: radix::radix_article
---

\newcommand{\C}{\mathbb{C}}
\newcommand{\R}{\mathbb{R}}
\newcommand{\Z}{\mathbb{Z}}
\newcommand{\N}{\mathbb{N}}
\newcommand{\<}{\left<}
\newcommand{\>}{\right>}
\newcommand{\ra}{\rightarrow}

# Examples of Banach space techniques

  - _5.2 Def:_ Consider the linear transformation $\Lambda:X\ra Y$ where $X$ and $Y$ are normed linear spaces. The norm of $\Lambda$ is given by
  
  $$\|\Lambda\|\equiv\sup\{\|\Lambda x\|:x\in X, \|x\|\leq1\}$$
